comparison_summary: Comparison of Our ORPO-Finetuned Model vs Baseline Mistral ORPO Variants
our_model: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct with ORPO finetuning on UltraFeedback

Alpha (lambda).description: Hybrid weight parameter controlling alpha token log odds weight

Beta (hybrid weight).description: Hybrid weight parameter balancing preference optimization

Log Odds Ratio.note: Negative indicates proper preference separation achieved

Token Log Odds.description: Per-token log odds - shows token-level preference learning

Sequence Log Odds.description: Sequence-level log odds - demonstrates sequence coherence

Positive Geometric Mean.description: Geometric mean of positive preference scores

Negative Geometric Mean.note: Close to positive mean indicates better preference discrimination

train/learning_rate.description: Reduced learning rate by end of training

train/grad_norm.note: Moderate gradient norm indicates stable training

model_comparison.efficiency_improvement: Our model achieves strong preference learning with 78% fewer parameters
model_comparison.training_convergence: Stable convergence over 10 epochs with consistent metrics

observations: Detailed and comprehensive responses with better structure
Better handling of complex multi-part questions
More contextually appropriate tone and language
Enhanced ability to provide step-by-step explanations

key_improvements_identified.improvement: Achieved separation between positive and negative preferences with controlled geometry
key_improvements_identified.evidence: Similar geometric means for positive/negative (-1.034 vs -0.992)

key_improvements_identified.improvement: Maintained stable gradient norm throughout training
key_improvements_identified.significance: Indicates no training instability or gradient explosion
Demonstrates detailed preference learning at token level

key_improvements_identified.improvement: Achieved competitive results with 78% fewer parameters than baseline
key_improvements_identified.note: Smaller model size with strong ORPO training outcomes

checkpoint_progression.note: Model saved at multiple checkpoints during training

metrics_tracking.note: Multiple metric exports showing detailed training progression

next_steps: Evaluate model outputs on held-out test set
Compare inference speed with baseline models
Fine-tune checkpoint selection based on validation metrics
Consider distillation to further reduce model size while maintaining performance
